# Generate a simple daily temperature sine curve

Generate a simple daily temperature sine curve

## Usage

``` r
dt_sin(
  dtr,
  tbar,
  sunrise = 6,
  resolution = 1,
  tempdecimalprecision = 1,
  clamp = TRUE
)
```

## Arguments

- dtr:

  Daily temperature range.

- tbar:

  A vector of target mean temperatures.

- sunrise:

  The time that the sun rises.

- resolution:

  The resolution of the time series in hours.

- clamp:

  Whether to clamp the output between `min(tbar)` and `max(tbar)`,
  defaults to `TRUE`.

## Value

A matrix of `24/resolution` (rows) x `length(tbar)` (cols) containing
temperature values at given times.

## Note

If `length(tbar) == 1`, clamp is automatically set to `FALSE`.

## Examples

``` r
dt_sin(dtr = 15, tbar = seq(20,45,0.1), sunrise = 6, resolution = 0.5, clamp = TRUE)
#>        20 20.1 20.2 20.3 20.4 20.5 20.6 20.7 20.8 20.9   21 21.1 21.2 21.3 21.4
```
